commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Ten fatted oxen, and twenty oxen out of the pastures, and a hundred sheep, beside harts, gazelles, roebucks, and fatted fowl. Yachmur means also a sort of wild-goat, like a stag, Deuteronomy xiv. (Bo chart, Anim. Some Rabbins explain barburim, (or borbrim) of capons, or birds from Barbary; as if this name had been known in the days of Solomon. (Calmet) There was an ancient Ethiopian Barbary on the Persian gulf, (Bo chart) with which the Rabbins were not acquainted.
